pick.rationale: Florida enters as one of the nation's elite programs, ranked 4th in both Torvik and KenPom systems with a dominant +33.83 efficiency margin compared to Lehigh's -10.38 rating—a staggering 44-point disparity that reflects elite offensive production and defensive dominance. The Gators' SEC pedigree combined with their 26-7 record creates an insurmountable advantage over Lehigh, a mid-major champion whose bottom-tier metrics simply cannot compete at this level. With no reported injuries affecting Florida's roster and historical precedent showing higher seeds winning 95%+ of matchups against lower seeds, this is a straightforward victory where the talent and system disparity across every metric makes any upset scenario virtually impossible.

reasoningStep.title: Florida vs Lehigh Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Florida's elite national ranking and massive efficiency margin advantage make them overwhelming favorites over Lehigh's bottom-tier program.
reasoningStep.evidence: Florida ranks 4th in both Torvik and KenPom with +33.83 efficiency margin versus Lehigh's 282nd/284th rankings and -10.38 efficiency margin. No significant injury concerns identified for either team during preliminary research.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Florida is the higher seed here (likely a 9 or 10 seed based on typical bracket structure), while Lehigh came through the First Four as a 16-seed.

Let me gather more information about both teams before making my pick. I should:

This is essentially a 1 vs 16 seed matchup where Florida is one of the nation's elite teams. The historical success rate of 1-seeds over 16-seeds is extremely high (over 95%+). Even if Florida isn't literally a #1 seed, they're clearly performing at that level based on their metrics.
